informal: Show that the countable collection \[\{(a, b) \times (c, d) \mid a < b \text{ and } c < d, \text{ and } a, b, c, d \text{ are rational}\}\] is a basis for $\mathbb{R}^2$.
reasoning: The informal statement asks to show the collection of products of open intervals with rational endpoints forms a topological basis for ℝ². The Lean signature asserts `IsTopologicalBasis rationalRectangleFamily`, which (assuming `rationalRectangleFamily` is defined as the family `{(a,b) × (c,d) | a<b, c<d, a,b,c,d ∈ ℚ}` of subsets of ℝ × ℝ with its standard topology) directly states exactly this. `IsTopologicalBasis` in Mathlib expresses "is a basis for the topology on the ambient type", which matches "is a basis for ℝ²". No extra hypotheses are added and no part of the conclusion is weakened. Conditional on the definition of `rationalRectangleFamily` being the obvious one, this is a faithful encoding.
